drugLibrary.title: Drug Library
drugLibrary.copy: Maintain curated local mappings and route extracted PK parameters through review before use.
drugLibrary.searchPlaceholder: Search generic name, brand name, alias, or Chinese keyword...
drugLibrary.unmappedChinese: No local mapping exists for this Chinese drug name yet. We can still resolve it through the full mapping table or openFDA fallback if available.
drugLibrary.remoteCopy: When no local registry row exists, we resolve aliases through the SQL mapping table, query openFDA with the canonical English term, and let you save a reviewed draft.
drugLibrary.remoteIdle: Enter a drug keyword to search the curated mapping table and openFDA candidates.
drugLibrary.remoteEmpty: No openFDA candidates matched this query.
drugLibrary.remoteUnmapped: This Chinese query is still unmapped in the SQL table. Add the alias to the mapping table before querying openFDA.

actions.deleteHistoryHint: Only records for this drug are removed

fdaPanel.title: FDA Search
fdaPanel.copy: Each drug resolves through the local curated mapping table before any openFDA NDC or label query runs. Extracted data never skips review.

safetyPanel.title: Safety and Alerts
safetyPanel.controlled: Controlled substances default to secondary confirmation and clinical review.
safetyPanel.standard: Standard drugs still retain local confirmation, history, and reminder policies.
safetyPanel.seedCount: The embedded registry currently ships with {count} reviewed or draftable drugs, and the plan column can save long-term templates directly.

source.recordsDescription: The latest {drug} record becomes T+0. Current anchor: {anchor}. If you change the recorded date or time, the curve recalculates from that timestamp.
source.scheduledDescription: Use a fixed dose, default interval, and steady-state or first-dose parameters to generate a demonstration curve.

warnings.injectionAssumption: Injection-route handling still uses provisional absorption assumptions for UI validation only. Do not treat it as a real exposure estimate.
warnings.noReferenceBand: This draft drug does not have a reviewed therapeutic window yet. The curve is still shown for structure testing only.
warnings.provisionalReferenceBand: The shaded window is provisional and has not been scientifically validated yet. Use it only as a review scaffold.

records.noRecords: No records yet. The curve stays at baseline.

chart.noSamples: No PK samples available for this chart yet.

courseTracker.title: Course Tracker
courseTracker.copy: Log each real administration, track cumulative dose, last-24-hour exposure, and adherence. All create or edit actions still use a second-confirmation step.
courseTracker.pkLinkNotice: These records directly drive the PK preview below. Any add, edit, or delete action recalculates the curve from the current log set.

form.sitePlaceholder: Example: left upper arm / prescribed site / clinician-administered
form.notePlaceholder: Record meal timing, delayed catch-up dosing, or prescription confirmation state

logs.title: Medication Logs
logs.empty: No medication logs exist for the current drug yet.

panels.aggregationCopy: Dose aggregation respects drug identity, formulation, route, course window, and log-time filtering. Different formulations do not collapse together unless an audited conversion rule exists.

confirm.copy: Re-check the administration details here. Confirming writes a factual log entry only and does not change the prescription itself.
confirm.safety: Controlled substances, psychiatric medications, sedatives, opioids, and injection logs should still be reviewed against the actual prescription and clinician instructions.

quickAdd.title: Long-term Template
quickAdd.copy: Pick a saved template and optionally override its parameters for this single log. The next step still goes through confirmation before anything is written.
quickAdd.notePlaceholder: Example: early dose / delayed dose / with meal / symptom note
quickAdd.noTemplate: No long-term template exists for the current drug yet. Save one from the library or PK preview before using quick add.

errors.noTemplate: No long-term template exists for the current drug yet.

oral.label: Oral
oral.summary: Follow the prescription, drug label, or pharmacist instructions. Never treat reminders as dosing advice.

items.item1: Confirm the drug name, strength, dosage form, intended dose, timing, and contraindication warnings.
items.item2: Do not crush, split, chew, or otherwise alter extended-release, controlled-release, enteric-coated, or sublingual formulations unless the official instructions allow it.
items.item3: If the prescription, label, clinician guidance, or current log disagree, stop and confirm before recording the dose.

items.item1: Only record what was actually taken and when it was taken. Do not use PK estimates as a reason to self-correct or increase a dose.
items.item2: Missed doses, vomiting, severe drowsiness, breathing changes, chest pain, altered consciousness, or allergic reactions should follow real medical guidance rather than local reminder logic.

injection.label: Injection
injection.summary: Only record injections already planned by a prescription or administered by qualified personnel. The app does not teach injection technique.

items.item1: Confirm the medication, concentration, dose, route, lot number, expiration, and order plan match.
items.item2: Use the sterile process defined by qualified medical guidance. Needles and syringes must be sterile, single-use, and single-person.
items.item3: Never reuse sharps, syringes, or injection supplies across administrations or people.

items.item1: Do not record the dose as administered if the solution is cloudy, discolored, damaged, leaking, or otherwise cannot be verified.
items.item2: Seek immediate medical help for severe pain, swelling, numbness, fever, breathing difficulty, rash, or significant bleeding.
items.item3: Dispose of sharps according to local medical-waste requirements rather than standard household trash.
